summary: Markets surged Friday as Intel posted a sixth consecutive earnings beat and its best day potentially since 1987, driving semiconductors to an 18th straight gain and powering the Nasdaq up 1.5% midday. The S&P 500 closed at a record 7,138 on Thursday before adding another 0.7% Friday, as 81% of S&P 500 companies beat earnings forecasts this season. However, consumer sentiment hit a record low of 49.8 in April despite ceasefire news, and Meta announced 10% workforce cuts totaling 8,000 employees amid AI spending pressures. The FOMC meets April 28-29 with Powell expected to hold rates at 3.5-3.75%, but markets are watching for any language acknowledging that falling oil could change the cut path. Bitcoin held near $77,600 as the $292 million KelpDAO hack rattled DeFi, while spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $2.1 billion in inflows over eight days. Oil volatility and Iran war headlines continue to inject uncertainty, but tech earnings strength and AI capex momentum are overpowering macro jitters for now.

signals.title: Intel earnings beat drives semiconductor rally to 18-day streak
signals.desc: Intel posted its sixth consecutive quarterly beat with upbeat Q2 guidance, triggering a potential best-day-since-1987 rally and lifting AMD +14%, Texas Instruments +18%, powering tech-heavy Nasdaq gains.

signals.title: S&P 500 earnings season delivers 81% beat rate
signals.desc: Strong corporate earnings with 81% of S&P 500 firms beating estimates and 76% topping revenue forecasts support equity markets despite geopolitical uncertainty and record-low consumer sentiment.

signals.title: Consumer sentiment hits record low at 49.8
signals.desc: University of Michigan final April reading of 49.8 marks the lowest on record—below financial crisis and pandemic levels—signaling persistent inflation fears despite Iran ceasefire extension.

signals.title: Meta cuts 10% of workforce as AI spending escalates
signals.desc: Meta confirmed 8,000 layoffs effective May 20 and scrapped 6,000 open roles as it redirects resources to AI infrastructure, signaling margin pressure across Big Tech despite strong revenue.

signals.title: $292 million KelpDAO hack rattles DeFi as Aave coordinates rescue
signals.desc: Year's biggest crypto theft hit Aave protocol, prompting industry-wide recovery effort and raising fresh questions about DeFi security ahead of institutional adoption; Bitcoin ETF inflows continue at $2.1B over eight days.

verdict: Tech earnings momentum and chip-led rally are overpowering consumer sentiment collapse and geopolitical noise, but FOMC language next week and oil volatility remain key swing factors.
